---
title: |
  Machine Learning for Characterization and Processing<br>Unit 12: Closed-Loop Experiment Control
bibliography: ref.bib
author:
  - name: Prof. Dr. Philipp Pelz
    affiliation:
      - FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg
execute:
  eval: false
format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    css: custom.css
    theme: custom.scss
    slide-number: c/t
    logo: "eclipse_logo_small.png"
    footer: "© Philipp Pelz - ML for Characterization and Processing"
---

## 01. Title: Closed-Loop Experiment Control
- Unit scope and role.

## 02. Learning outcomes
- Design safe adaptive experiment loops.

## 03. Recap from Unit 11
- Explainability + uncertainty decision gates.

## 04. Closed-loop system architecture
- Sense -> infer -> decide -> act.

## 05. Components and interfaces
- Instruments, ML, controller, operator.

## 06. Control objectives
- Quality, throughput, safety.

## 07. State estimation basics
- Observable vs latent process states.

## 08. Feedback vs feedforward control
- Complementary control modes.

## 09. ML role in control stack
- Prediction, anomaly detection, policy support.

## 10. Surrogate-assisted control
- Fast approximations for decision loops.

## 11. Uncertainty-aware action selection
- Risk-bounded decisions.

## 12. Safety constraints and guardrails
- Hard limits and interlocks.

## 13. Human override mechanisms
- Authority and escalation design.

## 14. Latency requirements
- Timing budgets in control loops.

## 15. Robustness to noise/drift
- Stable behavior under change.

## 16. OOD handling in closed loop
- Detect and fail safely.

## 17. Reward/objective shaping
- Utility design in practice.

## 18. Model predictive control intuition
- Receding-horizon decisions.

## 19. Bayesian optimization in loop
- Sample-efficient policy improvement.

## 20. Reinforcement learning caution (light)
- Data efficiency and safety limitations.

## 21. Sim2real workflow
- Simulation pre-validation before deployment.

## 22. Digital twin concept
- Virtual process companion.

## 23. Online learning policy
- When and how to update models.

## 24. Monitoring and alerting
- Health metrics and trigger rules.

## 25. Logging and traceability
- Auditability of actions.

## 26. Incident response
- Recovery and rollback procedures.

## 27. Validation protocol
- Offline, shadow, phased rollout.

## 28. KPI dashboard
- Performance/safety observability.

## 29. Case sketch: autonomous microscopy acquisition
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 30. Case sketch: adaptive process tuning
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 31. Case sketch: spectroscopy-guided control
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 32. Failure mode #1
- Feedback instability.

## 33. Failure mode #2
- Confidently wrong model actions.

## 34. Failure mode #3
- Silent drift and delayed detection.

## 35. Mitigation checklist
- Technical + procedural safeguards.

## 36. Governance and accountability
- Roles and sign-off model.

## 37. Regulatory/quality context
- Lab and industrial constraints.

## 38. Bridge to Unit 13
- End-to-end capstone integration.

## 39. Exercise scaffold: loop diagram
- Define system interfaces and data flow.

## 40. Exercise scaffold: policy design
- Objective + constraints + fallback.

## 41. Exercise scaffold: simulation test
- Validate stability and safety.

## 42. Exercise scaffold: uncertainty gating
- Implement abstain/escalate rules.

## 43. Exercise scaffold: monitoring dashboard
- Define key runtime indicators.

## 44. Exercise scaffold: incident drill
- Post-mortem workflow.

## 45. Exam-oriented key statements
- High-yield concepts.

## 46. Summary table
- Methods, benefits, risks.

## 47. Unit summary
- Core takeaways.

## 48. Reading assignment
- Final unit capstone prep.

## 49. References
- Source list.

## 50. Q&A buffer slide
- Discussion and synthesis.

::: {#refs}
:::
